purpose: Defines the !onboard slash command — produces a comprehensive project orientation: what it does, how it's structured, how to run things, key architectural decisions, and where to find what. Two modes: !onboard for full walkthrough, !onboard {area} for area-specific deep dive (e.g., 'api', 'frontend', 'auth'). Reads .synapse/toc.md, CLAUDE.md, package.json/tsconfig.json/etc., and .env.example to source content. Outputs Project Overview / Structure table / Architecture / How Things Connect / Quick Start / Key Conventions / Available Commands / Environment Setup / Where to Find Things sections. Designed for new sessions or new contributors. Read-only.
gotchas: Source EVERYTHING from actual files — don't invent setup steps; read them from CLAUDE.md, package.json, README.md.
Keep it ACTIONABLE — a new contributor should be able to start working after reading the output.
Two output structures: full walkthrough (!onboard) vs deep-dive (!onboard {area}) — they share no template.
patterns: Source-of-truth-first: read actual files, never invent.
conventions: Quick-start steps sourced from CLAUDE.md or package.json.
